name: Poranguí
bio: Brazilian-born multi-instrumentalist Poranguí weaves ancestral chants, live-looped percussion, and Amazonian field recordings into ceremonial medicine music. From his Sedona sanctuary, he summons a one-man orchestra of didgeridoo, guitar, flutes, and voice.
offerings: Live-looping world-soul performances, sound healing ceremonies, retreats, and recorded albums; also offers bodywork in Sedona.
touring: Actively touring. A 2026 run (approx. Mar 21 – Sep 4) spans Brazil (São Paulo, Florianópolis) and the US. See porangui.com/events.

name: Madame Gandhi
bio: She drums her way into the icy quiet, weaving pulse and breath into a feminist electronic prayer. Here, liberation sounds like a heartbeat and the earth breathes back.
offerings: Albums and EPs of percussive feminist electronic music, plus live shows, DJ sets, and speaking engagements.
touring: Actively touring 2025–2026. Lincoln Center, NYC (Jul 8, 2026); National Sawdust Spring Equinox, NYC (Mar 20, 2026); UK dates.

name: Deya Dova
bio: Deya Dova channels the Earth grid through her voice. The Australian songwoman records live at sacred sites across the planet, weaving ancient songlines and geomantic frequencies into a ceremonial vocal medicine that pulses with global bass.
offerings: Global-bass world-electronic music including the Planetary Grid Music Collection recorded live at 33+ sacred sites; concert tickets.
touring: Active international touring artist based in Byron Bay, Australia. Festival and live dates via Bandsintown / Songkick.

name: Yaima
bio: Cascadian folktronic ceremony conjured by vocalist Pepper Proud and multi-instrumentalist Masaru Higasa. Ancestral breath meets electronic pulse, weaving world-rooted song, elemental reverence, and transcendent chant into a living bridge between nature and the human heart.
offerings: Albums and singles (Pellucidity, OvO) plus live folktronic concert tours; music on Bandcamp and Spotify.
touring: Actively touring 2026. Spring western-US run (Mar 29 – Apr 15): Tucson, Flagstaff, Durango, Albuquerque, Salt Lake City, La Jolla, LA, Ventura, Berkeley, Felton.

name: The Human Experience
bio: David Block composes live electronic symphonies, layering prismatic soundscapes over sultry beats. Multi-instrumentalist and producer, he summons ceremony from circuitry — sacred sound engineered for bodies in transformation.
offerings: Live electronic performances and recorded music across projects (The Human Experience, Two Tigers, Gone Gone Beyond); the 'Lost Art of Listening' offering.
touring: Actively touring. Upcoming billed dates via Bandsintown / Songkick.

name: Gone Gone Beyond
bio: A folk-electronic collective led by David Block (The Human Experience) with Danny Musengo, Kat Factor, and Mel Semé, weaving acoustic harmonies and cinematic bass into what they call mesmerizing future folk for the transformational awakening.
offerings: 'Future folk' albums and tracks blending acoustic, world and electronic elements; touring live band.
touring: Actively touring. Next dates cited at SOMO Village, Rohnert Park CA and Hutchinson Ranch, Salida CO (Songkick).

name: Equanimous
bio: Equanimous — the vessel-name of Nate Stein, revealed to him in silence at a Vipassana retreat — weaves djembe, live keys, and beatbox into sacred bass. From Nevada City, he conjures heart-opening electronic ceremony for the conscious dance floor.
offerings: Downtempo-bass and conscious-electronic music via Bandcamp / streaming; runs High Vibe Records; live sets with live looping.
touring: Actively touring; an Australia run is aggregator-listed (Sydney, Coburg). Dates via Songkick.

name: Anilah
bio: Anilah chants medicine into being. Her shamanic throat-song, breath, and elemental rhythm weave a sonic ceremony — where voice becomes the thread that stitches body to earth to spirit.
offerings: Albums/EPs (Warrior, Chrysalis) of ritual vocal sound-medicine via Bandcamp and streaming; featured on History Channel's Vikings.
touring: Has a Bandsintown profile; no specific public dates confirmed.

name: Torkom Ji
bio: Torkom Ji weaves live 432Hz synthesis into ceremonial sound journeys, coaxing listeners into deep theta states through his proprietary Quantum Harmonix system. From Los Angeles domes to global transformational festivals, he conducts harmonic fields that dissolve the thinking mind into still, luminous presence.
offerings: Live 432Hz Quantum Harmonix sound baths, recorded albums, and sound design; music on Bandcamp.
touring: Performs frequently at festivals (Burning Man, Lightning in a Bottle, Bhakti Fest) and LA venues (Wisdome.LA, Unplug).

name: Liquid Bloom
bio: Liquid Bloom weaves tribal-electronic ceremony from field-recorded voices, earthy percussion, and world instrumentation. The project carries ancestral rhythms into modern sound design, opening sonic temples for trance, meditation, and collective resonance.
offerings: World-electronic / downtempo ceremony music (Desert Trax) plus live concerts and breathwork-ceremony performances.
touring: Actively touring. Confirmed: concert + breathwork ceremony, Thu May 14, 2026 at Third Room, Asheville NC.

name: Mei-lan
bio: Mei-lan Maurits is an American sound oracle and intuitive vocalist whose ethereal voice channels sacred song-medicine for healing and awakening. Through live sound journeys, guided meditations, and light-language transmissions, she bridges higher frequencies into the body.
offerings: Live sound-healing performances, guided meditations, online courses, and a music subscription app; hosts the 'Soul Code Transmissions' podcast.
touring: Works largely through online / app offerings; appears at events such as the Global Unity Festival. No specific upcoming concert dates confirmed.

name: Taylor Sol
bio: Taylor Sol is a sound alchemist and neo-shamanic facilitator from Gisborne, Aotearoa New Zealand, weaving 432 Hz handpan, Native American flute, taonga pūoro, and crystal bowls into ceremonial soundscapes that return the listener to the body.
offerings: Live handpan concerts and sound journeys, 1:1 lessons, private sound healings, mentorship, and an app 'Alchemy with Taylor Sol'.
touring: Plays live handpan concerts including a '432Hz / The Miracle Tone' series in Auckland NZ.

name: Vianney Lopez
bio: Vianney Lopez carries the Native American flute as breath, prayer, and offering. From San Antonio, Texas, she braids Wixarika, Raramuri, and Taino lineage into ceramic instruments and ancestral songs for the Beloved Earth.
offerings: Handmade ceramic instruments, Native American flute releases, plus online and in-person workshops and retreats.
touring: Hosts dated retreats: a Women's Ceramic & Music Retreat (June 2026) and a 4-day Ceramic Instrument Studio Immersion.

name: Sam Bottner
bio: Chicago-based producer, sound journeyer, and breathwork facilitator, Sam Bottner sculpts live-music ceremonies where organic strings, voice, and electronic ambience carry the listener into expanded states.
offerings: Live-music breathwork ceremonies (the 'Thrive' experience) plus sonic journeys and recorded music; yoga & meditation teacher.
touring: Centered on Chicago events (Thrive breathwork, Ecstatic Dance Chicago). No public ticketed tour dates found.

name: Musical Mindset
bio: Musical Mindset weaves native flute, shamanic drum, and throat song into living ceremony — Paul Grosso's live-looped rituals braid Greek roots and Indigenous medicine into soundscapes that open the heart and remember the body.
offerings: Live-looping performances and sound journeys, one-on-one acoustic sound healings, retreats, and live events.
touring: Listed as a retreat leader on retreat.guru; performs live sound-healing events (largely Chicago-area).

name: Amaruka Sol
bio: Colombian-American medicine rapper and rose priestess rooted in Nevada City, California. Her voice braids Spanish invocations, ancestral prayer, and hip-hop bars into ceremonial sound — feminine, sovereign, unapologetic.
offerings: Conscious / medicine hip-hop releases (solo and as 'Winged Cobra') plus 'Hip Hop Medicine' label work and rose-priestess offerings.
touring: Performs at ceremonial / festival events; no publicized tour calendar found.

name: Rob Lowman
bio: Rob Lowman is a Nashville-based cellist and composer weaving classical discipline into cinematic, slow-moving textures. His strings move like breath — arranged, layered, and rendered for collaborators from Kehlani to Josh Garrels, and for meditations that name themselves after emptiness.
offerings: Session cellist / composer / arranger services plus his own releases (e.g. 'Between Worlds'); available for hire.
touring: Primarily a session and touring sideman rather than a headlining act. No public 2026 dates found.

name: Liesbet Leroy
bio: A Dutch multi-instrumentalist and composer, she channels Native American flutes, handpan, and ambient soundscapes into meditative, breath-led music. Every note arrives intuitively, a ceremony of stillness stitched from world instruments and nature's own voice.
offerings: Meditative / ambient and healing music (Native flute + world instruments) via Bandcamp and Spotify, including label releases.
touring: Primarily a recording / composing artist; no dated tour schedule found.

name: Jade Fusco
bio: Jade Fusco sings as DMZL from Austin, Texas — a Mistress of Ceremonial Arts who opens the throat as a portal, weaving jazz, vocal alchemy and ritual theater into prayers that summon the ancestors back into the room.
offerings: Ceremonial / ritual-theater music and performance, a 'Communing with the Muse' course, and EFT-based singing mentorship; also a muralist.
touring: Austin TX-based performer; no public tour dates found.

name: Kestenbetsa
bio: Kestenbetsa is the Shipibo name of Guillermo Arevalo Valera, a maestro curandero born in Yarinacocha, Peru in 1952. His voice carries icaros taught by the plants, songs that weave geometric light through ayahuasca ceremony.
offerings: Recorded icaros (Shipibo healing chants); runs ayahuasca healing retreat centers in Peru (Espíritu de Anaconda, Bena Shinan).
touring: A maestro curandero, not a touring musician; runs retreat centers in Peru.

name: Liria Rose
bio: Austin-based sound healer and classically trained flutist who weaves Native flutes, singing bowls, voice, and hand drum into meditative journeys — a practice born after her father's passing opened a path from concert stage to healing temple.
offerings: In-person and private sound baths (flutes, crystal bowls, hand drum, voice), corporate and retreat wellness events, a Native-style flute course, and a 'Sound of Love' practitioner certification.
touring: Hosts recurring sound baths around Austin, TX; events listed via Eventbrite. No standing tour.

name: Mystic Transmission
bio: A cinematic post-rock current from Northern California. Garrett Bevins and Beau Askew move past the shore break into ambient, instrumental vastness — slow guitars, soundtrack air, tracks that sit comfortably beneath meditation and breath. (The duo records as Beyond the Waves; 'Mystic Transmission' is one of their releases.)
offerings: Instrumental cinematic post-rock / ambient recordings (e.g. 'Mystic Transmission', 'Levitation'); Garrett Bevins also licenses music for film and video.
touring: A studio recording project rather than an active touring act.

name: Origins Curriculum
bio: The world's first Conscious Curriculum — a PreK-12 framework weaving eco-literacy, mindfulness, and academics into seasonal rhythms. Their kids' meditations help young hearts tune inward.
offerings: A secular PreK-12 'Conscious Curriculum' subscription — lesson plans, workbooks, and a library of movement, music, and guided-meditation videos for classrooms and homeschool.
touring: An education organization, not a touring artist. Year-long PreK-5 curricula, supplemental units for grades 6-12, and a homeschool line.
